result.chapter_summary: Change is possible through the implementation of a knowledge-coherent curriculum, as evidenced by dramatic reading gains in Sullivan County, Tennessee, and the South Bronx. However, progress is hindered by entrenched ideological convictions in education schools and district leadership, requiring parents and citizens to pressure state legislatures for specific curriculum reforms.

claims.text: Public schools are entrenched institutions that cannot change overnight.
claims.source_passage: In truth, they could not change overnight even if they wanted to.

claims.text: The low international ranking of American schools (24th in the world) threatens the nation's future prosperity.
claims.source_passage: How much longer can we prosper when our schools now rank twenty-fourth in the world?

claims.text: State legislatures must establish specific curriculum requirements rather than vague guidelines.
claims.source_passage: But ultimately, states will need to get specific. Patriotic citizens need to unite to inform our state legislatures, enlighten their minds, and stiffen their spines.

claims.text: Elementary schools can begin improving immediately by adopting the methods of successful principals and teachers from disadvantaged neighborhoods.
claims.source_passage: On their own initiative, our elementary schools can start improving overnight, now that we are able to introduce parents, teachers, and principals to instant videos of top principals and teachers who can explain exactly how they achieved their thrilling successes in severely disadvantaged neighborhoods

claims.text: Successful educational practitioners have generally failed to change the minds or behaviors of district-level administrators.
claims.source_passage: But effective as these teachers and principals have been in achieving what they set out to do in educating young children, they have been unsuccessful in changing the minds and the behavior of the adults in charge of their school districts.

claims.text: School districts are trapped in a 'web of ideas' dominated by the ideological convictions of education professors.
claims.source_passage: That is because our districts have been trapped in a web of ideas and slogans that still govern the minds of our education professors and hence the minds of teachers and administrators.

claims.text: The dominant ideas in American education schools reflect a powerful, quasi-religious conviction that 'nature knows best.'
claims.source_passage: Those ideas endure powerfully because they reflect at bottom a powerful religious conviction that “nature knows best.”

claims.text: Empirical results from impoverished children consistently achieving top results disprove the 'natural development' theories of John Dewey and education schools.
claims.source_passage: nature isn’t really saying what John Dewey and our schools of education thought it was saying. These kids prove that we can turn this around and get all students on a level playing field.

claims.text: A knowledge-coherent curriculum based on shared literate knowledge is the most effective way to raise student competence.
claims.source_passage: These stories show what can be accomplished in raising student competence by teaching a knowledge-coherent curriculum based on the knowledge that tends to be shared among literate Americans.

claims.text: Significant reading gains in Sullivan County, Tennessee, were directly caused by the introduction of the Core Knowledge Sequence and teacher training.
claims.source_passage: The gains are by far the largest ever recorded in the district... They were achieved by introducing a language arts program created by the Core Knowledge Foundation, formed from the Core Knowledge Sequence of shared topics.

claims.text: Progress made in one disadvantaged region, like rural Appalachia, can be replicated in any other location.
claims.source_passage: If this kind of progress can be made in Appalachia by an entire district, it can be made anywhere.

claims.text: By eighth grade, students with a coherent knowledge-based curriculum can reach a level of proficiency equivalent to top-performing students in debate and academic competition.
claims.source_passage: By eighth grade, with a coherent knowledge-based curriculum in all subjects, these children, just like Litt’s students in the South Bronx, could be winning debate contests in Memphis or Richmond

claims.text: A curriculum in the hands of teachers committed to student growth meets the needs of all learners.
claims.source_passage: The curriculum in the hands of teachers committed to student growth meets the needs of all learners.

claims.text: Core Knowledge Language Arts (CKLA) is designed to engage students with science and history topics while building background knowledge through reading.
claims.source_passage: The curriculum, Core Knowledge Language Arts or CKLA, is designed to engage students with rich science and history topics while they grow as readers who are building background knowledge.

claims.text: Fixing educational problems requires significant investment in both a new curriculum and the teacher training necessary for its implementation.
claims.source_passage: This district recognized a problem and made a significant commitment to fix it—by investing in a new curriculum and the teacher training needed to implement it.

claims.text: Educational progress achieved in Appalachia proves that similar results are possible in any school district.
claims.source_passage: If this kind of progress can be made in Appalachia by an entire district, it can be made anywhere.

claims.text: Children from any background can succeed in high-level intellectual competitions when provided with a coherent knowledge-based curriculum.
claims.source_passage: By eighth grade, with a coherent knowledge-based curriculum in all subjects, these children, just like Litt’s students in the South Bronx, could be winning debate contests in Memphis or Richmond

claims.text: Every first-rate education system in the world utilizes an agreed-on national sequence of core topics in elementary school.
claims.source_passage: No first-rate education system in the world evades the logic of an agreed-on national sequence of core topics in elementary school.

claims.text: Evading the logic of a national core sequence is a self-defeating policy for a nation.
claims.source_passage: It is self-defeating to evade this logic, which represents a kind of unilateral intellectual disarmament in a competitive world.

claims.text: The refusal to adopt a common core of knowledge constitutes a form of unilateral intellectual disarmament.
claims.source_passage: It is self-defeating to evade this logic, which represents a kind of unilateral intellectual disarmament in a competitive world.

claims.text: Future generations will judge the current generation harshly if they fail to act on current knowledge of educational effectiveness.
claims.source_passage: Now that we have the knowledge, future generations will find it unforgivable that we did nothing

claims.text: The greatness of the United States is historically rooted in its combination of idealism and pragmatism.
claims.source_passage: particularly as we are a nation whose greatness owes itself to our idealism and our pragmatism.

claims.text: Numerous schools have overcome statistical odds and disadvantaged circumstances by adopting shared-knowledge curricula.
claims.source_passage: We have example after example of schools bucking the odds because they’ve turned to a shared-knowledge curriculum

claims.text: Disadvantaged students using a shared-knowledge curriculum are capable of outperforming students from the most advantaged school districts.
claims.source_passage: the disadvantaged students who beat out the most advantaged districts in New York and New Jersey at the American Debate League Middle School City Championships.

claims.text: A shared-knowledge curriculum increases student confidence and excitement about learning.
claims.source_passage: I’ve listened to these students talk about what this has done for their confidence and their excitement about learning

claims.text: The United States requires an educational revolution to implement shared-knowledge standards nationally.
claims.source_passage: I hope that their message can also deeply impress school administrators, teachers, parents, and voters in every school district in the nation, because it’s time for an educational revolution!

claims.text: Parents must build local coalitions with receptive teachers, principals, and fellow citizens to effect change in entrenched public school institutions.
claims.source_passage: You as a parent will need the support of receptive teachers and principals. You will need the support of fellow citizens in your location.

claims.text: Patriotic citizens have a duty to unite and exert pressure on state legislatures to ensure they adopt specific, rather than vague, educational standards.
claims.source_passage: Patriotic citizens need to unite to inform our state legislatures, enlighten their minds, and stiffen their spines.

claims.text: There exists a specific body of knowledge that is generally shared among literate Americans which can serve as the foundation for school curricula.
claims.source_passage: These stories show what can be accomplished in raising student competence by teaching a knowledge-coherent curriculum based on the knowledge that tends to be shared among literate Americans.

claims.text: The Core Knowledge Foundation provides free downloadable materials specifically as an emergency intervention for schools willing to adopt their sequence.
claims.source_passage: Our Core Knowledge Foundation is tiny, but as an emergency measure it can supply your school with free downloadable materials if it wants to take the leap.

claims.text: Elementary schools possess the capacity to begin their improvement process immediately through the use of instructional videos featuring successful practitioners.
claims.source_passage: On their own initiative, our elementary schools can start improving overnight, now that we are able to introduce parents, teachers, and principals to instant videos of top principals and teachers who can explain exactly how they achieved their thrilling successes

claims.text: The educational philosophies that dominate districts endure primarily because they are anchored in a deep-seated religious-like conviction regarding nature's inherent wisdom.
claims.source_passage: Those ideas endure powerfully because they reflect at bottom a powerful religious conviction that “nature knows best.”

evidence.description: US schools currently rank twenty-fourth in the world in international comparisons.
Video evidence documenting the successes of principals and teachers in severely disadvantaged neighborhoods.
Sullivan County, Tennessee, screening data showing a decrease in 'at-risk' readers from 37.8% to 26% in one year.
evidence.source_reference: Robin McClellan, Kingsport, Tennessee, Times News

evidence.description: Data from Bluff City Elementary School showing 30% fewer students at-risk in kindergarten and 20% fewer in first grade after the new curriculum.
evidence.source_reference: Robin McClellan, Kingsport, Tennessee, Times News

evidence.description: Increase in Sullivan County students scoring in the 75-100th percentile band from 15.2% to 22.1% following curriculum implementation.
evidence.source_reference: Robin McClellan, Kingsport, Tennessee, Times News

evidence.description: A district-wide implementation of CKLA and teacher training in Appalachia resulting in significant student progress.
The success of Litt’s students in the South Bronx using a knowledge-based curriculum.
Global performance of educational systems that utilize a national sequence of core topics.
Results of the American Debate League Middle School City Championships where disadvantaged students defeated advantaged districts.

concepts.author_definition: A curriculum based on the specific knowledge that tends to be shared among literate Americans, sequenced to build background knowledge across subjects.
An ideological slogan/religious conviction that children's learning should follow their innate, natural development rather than a communal curriculum.
Core Knowledge Language Arts; a curriculum designed to build background knowledge through science and history topics while teaching literacy.
The author's term for a nation's refusal to adopt a shared, standardized core of knowledge, which weakens its competitive position globally and fails its students.
A proposed shift across all school districts toward shared-knowledge curricula and common standards of content.
The reference group whose shared background knowledge forms the basis of a 'knowledge-coherent curriculum' capable of raising student competence.
The provision of free materials by the Core Knowledge Foundation to circumvent the slow pace of institutional change in public school districts.
A metric of reading proficiency used to identify students performing below national benchmarks, which the author argues can be rapidly reduced through curriculum change.

cases.name: Sullivan County, Tennessee
cases.description: A rural Appalachian district with 60% economically deprived students that implemented CKLA. Within a few months, the district saw the largest reading gains in its history, significantly reducing at-risk readers and increasing the number of students in the highest percentile bands across all 11 elementary schools.
key_passages: The district achieved a significant decrease in the number of students with “at-risk” levels of reading proficiency, from 37.8% of students in the fall screening to 26% in the spring screening.
The gains are by far the largest ever recorded in the district, and they are remarkable, coming as they are after just a few months.

cases.name: South Bronx Schools (Jeffrey Litt)
cases.description: A network of seven schools in the heart of the South Bronx that achieved 'astonishing' results by teaching a knowledge-coherent curriculum to disadvantaged students.
key_passages: followed by an account of Jeffrey Litt’s astonishing seven schools in the heart of the South Bronx.
these children, just like Litt’s students in the South Bronx, could be winning debate contests

cases.name: Appalachia School District
cases.description: An entire school district in the Appalachian region that committed to fixing educational deficits by investing in CKLA and intensive teacher training, serving as a model for universal scalability.
key_passages: If this kind of progress can be made in Appalachia by an entire district, it can be made anywhere.

cases.name: South Bronx Debate Success
cases.description: Disadvantaged students from the South Bronx who, through a knowledge-rich education, were able to win citywide debate championships against students from high-wealth districts in New York and New Jersey.
key_passages: the disadvantaged students who beat out the most advantaged districts in New York and New Jersey at the American Debate League Middle School City Championships.

thinkers.name: John Dewey
thinkers.key_idea: The idea that educational development should follow a child's natural instincts rather than a structured cultural curriculum.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch argues that empirical evidence from successful knowledge-based schools proves Dewey's assumptions about 'nature' were incorrect.
key_passages: nature isn’t really saying what John Dewey and our schools of education thought it was saying.

actors.name: Core Knowledge Foundation
actors.context: Supplies free downloadable materials and the Core Knowledge Sequence as an emergency measure for schools.

actors.name: Robin McClellan
actors.context: Elementary supervisor for Sullivan County who reported the district's historic reading gains.

actors.name: Christy Nelson
actors.context: K–5 instructional coach who noted exponential growth in students moving out of at-risk designations.

actors.name: Education Professors
actors.context: Accused of governing the minds of teachers and administrators with slogans and religious-like convictions about naturalism.

actors.name: Litt
actors.context: A teacher in the South Bronx whose students achieved academic success through a knowledge-based curriculum.

actors.name: American Debate League
actors.context: The organization that hosts the middle school city championships used as evidence of student performance.

objections_raised.objection: Parents of children in public schools feel hopeless to change entrenched educational institutions.
objections_raised.response: While institutions cannot change overnight, success stories from disadvantaged districts and the availability of free materials prove that change is possible through curriculum reform and political pressure.

cross_chapter_refs.reference_text: I began this book with very encouraging descriptions of regular public schools in Alexandria, Virginia, and in Riverside, California
